_comment: SkillSpector suppression baseline — Phase K (loopclose_3005). Generated 2026-06-02.
_format: rule_id -> list of 'file:line' patterns or '*' (suppress all locations). Entries are verified false positives only.

_rationale.P2/asciinema-demo.svg:20: SVG HTML comments in a demo animation file; not AI-readable instruction plane. Not a real prompt injection vector.
_rationale.TM1/QUICKSTART-share.md:25: DELETE HTTP method in documentation example. REST docs showing the full CRUD surface; no actual shell execution.
_rationale.TM1/mcp/03-cookbook-share.sh:10: DELETE in shell script comment line. Comment block describing REST endpoints, never executed as a shell command.
_rationale.E1/QUICKSTART-publisher.md:58: curl POST to recipes.wisechef.ai in publisher quickstart docs. Legitimate first-party API call to owned endpoint.
_rationale.E1/mcp/03-cookbook-share.sh:37: curl POST to ${BASE_URL}/api/cookbooks/ — base URL is user-supplied, calling our own API. Legitimate example code.
_rationale.SC2/mcp/03-cookbook-share.sh:*: curl calls piped to `python3 -m json.tool` for pretty-printing JSON. Not executing remote code — using stdlib json.tool as a formatter. curl responses are JSON, not scripts.
_rationale.TT3/rest/*: Example REST API clients reading RECIPES_API_KEY from env and sending it as an Authorization header to recipes.wisechef.ai (our own API). This is the intended pattern for API authentication, not credential exfiltration.
_rationale.E2/rest/*: Same as TT3 — os.environ.get('RECIPES_API_KEY') is the correct way to read an API key for authenticating against our own service in example code.
